pilots.description: Measure absorption prevalence on 100 sequences, layer 8, 8K dict. Compute absorption score for all latents.
pilots.pass_criteria: At least 5% of latents show absorption score >0.5 (signal detectable); random control shows <2%
pilots.falsification_action: If <5% absorption: H1 likely falsified; skip full experiment and focus on H2/H5

pilots.description: Correlate token frequency with absorption score on 100 sequences, layer 8.
pilots.pass_criteria: Spearman r < -0.1 between log-frequency and absorption score; lowest-frequency bin mean > highest-frequency bin mean
pilots.falsification_action: If r >= 0 or no quartile difference: H2 falsified; skip full experiment

pilots.description: Proxy sparsity-absorption relationship via L0 correlation across available checkpoints, layer 8.
pilots.pass_criteria: Monotonic trend detectable: higher L0 (sparser) correlates with higher absorption; or mini-trained SAEs show ordered absorption rates
pilots.falsification_action: If no monotonic trend or negative correlation: H3 falsified; skip full experiment

pilots.hypothesis: H4: Downstream Impact on Circuit Discovery
pilots.description: Activation patching on one factual recall task at layer 8. Compare raw residual vs. SAE latent patching.
pilots.pass_criteria: Faithfulness score computable and >0 for raw residual; SAE patching within 20 pp of raw
pilots.falsification_action: If SAE patching is degenerate (faithfulness <0 or >30 pp worse than raw): debug methodology before full experiment

pilots.description: Compare absorption rates for 2K vs. 8K dictionary sizes at layer 8 on 100 sequences.
pilots.pass_criteria: 8K dict shows lower mean absorption than 2K dict; random controls scale appropriately
pilots.falsification_action: If 8K >= 2K absorption or random controls do not scale: H5 falsified; skip full experiment

rationale: H1 pilot runs first because it establishes the core metric and validates signal detectability. H2, H3, H5 are independent analytical pilots that can run in parallel after H1. H4 runs last because it depends on identifying low- vs. high-absorption layers from H1 results.
